info.name: Tourism API
info.description:
REST API for the tourism booking platform.

This collection is GENERATED. Do NOT edit `docs/postman/tourism-api.json` directly — edit the source files in `docs/postman/src/` and run `pnpm postman:build` instead. See `docs/en/runbooks/postman-auth.md` for the build workflow.

Folder source files live in `docs/postman/src/folders/` (one file per top-level folder). The build script merges them in the order listed by `folders` below.

exec: // Auto-refresh Supabase access token if expired or missing.
// Skipped for the Health folder (no auth required).
// Reset the generic Bearer slot up-front. If we fail to pick a valid
// per-role token below, the request goes out with an empty Bearer and
// the backend returns a clear 401 — instead of silently reusing a
// leftover token from a previous run (e.g. admin token leaking into
// fall back to customer credentials otherwise. Manual override via
// Per-role slots are deliberately distinct from the generic
// `accessToken` Bearer slot. Earlier versions used `accessToken` as
// BOTH the customer slot AND the generic Bearer slot, so any admin
// fetch would silently overwrite the customer's cached token.
console.warn('[pre-request] Supabase credentials missing for ' + (isAdmin ? 'admin' : 'customer') + ' — protected requests will fail with 401.');
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ email: email, password: password }) }
if (err) { console.error('[pre-request] Supabase auth failed', err); return; }
console.error('[pre-request] No access_token in response', body);
